title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. font-queri
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. font-queri, commonly known as the Kermes oak kidney vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney vetch plant that belongs to the legume family. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations, offering potential health benefits such as improved skin health and reduced risk of infection. Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. font-queri can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
